fresh_facts.claim: The US maintains an ongoing naval blockade targeting Iranian ports and coastal areas to restrict oil exports and apply economic pressure.
Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warned that access to the Strait of Hormuz depends on Tehran's authorization if US pressure continues.
IRGC naval forces intercepted two US warships in the Strait of Hormuz, locking 16 cruise missiles on them and ordering retreat to the Indian Ocean.
A fragile ceasefire between Iran and the US is set to expire around April 22-30, 2026.
US President Trump threatened to resume strikes if no deal is reached by April 22, 2026.
Initial ceasefire talks in Islamabad on April 11 collapsed due to internal Iranian disagreements and orders from top security officials.
President Masoud Pezeshkian faces fierce hardline backlash for a conditional war-end offer and ceasefire acceptance.
The IRGC has assumed control over key functions, pushing Pezeshkian into deadlock and pressured him to appoint Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as Supreme National Security Council secretary.
A Provisional Leadership Council consisting of Pezeshkian, judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni-Ejei, and cleric Alireza Arafi holds interim power amid a leadership crisis.
The Assembly of Experts delays supreme leader succession, with Mojtaba Khamenei positioned as the logical heir.
Iran's central bank warned Pezeshkian that war-damaged economy rebuilding could take over a decade.
Iran's political stability index hit -1.58 in 2024, the weakest on record and far below the world average of -0.07.
The Strait of Hormuz handles approximately 20% of global crude oil flows.
Iran shut the Strait of Hormuz after accusing the US of ceasefire violations.
Hardliners labeled ex-Foreign Minister Zarif a 'traitor' for war-end calls, urging arrests of him and ex-President Rouhani.
US warships USS Michael Murphy and USS Frank E. Peterson Jr. are conducting high-risk maneuvers in the Strait of Hormuz.
Pakistan is mediating talks between the US and Iran after failed US-Iran negotiations.
Iran is considering a temporary halt on Hormuz shipments to ease the US blockade.
State media criticized Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi for confusing statements that allegedly aid Trump.
The ceasefire does not represent a formal deal between Iran and the US.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces an imminent April 22 deadline for resumed US strikes with a fragile ceasefire that does not constitute a formal deal, while the regime experiences severe internal fracturing between President Pezeshkian and hardline IRGC forces, compounded by a decade-long economic reconstruction outlook and threats to close the Strait of Hormuz (20% of global crude). A Provisional Leadership Council now holds interim power amid succession uncertainty, with second-round ceasefire talks scheduled in Islamabad as Pakistan mediates negotiations.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US naval blockade on Iranian ports continues with high-risk warship maneuvers in the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran shut the strait after accusing the US of ceasefire violations and parliament speaker Ghalibaf warned that Hormuz access depends on Tehran's authorization, creating a critical confrontation timeline before the April 22 deadline.
